jobs.title: Head of Local Financial Regulatory Reporting (UAE Nationals Only)
jobs.description: The Finance Reporting Lead Analyst is senior professional position responsible for driving the generation, analysis, and submission of reports to various constituencies based on local IFRS standards and/ or US Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP). local statutory reporting, in coordination with the broader Financial Reporting Team. The overall objective is to apply in-depth disciplinary knowledge to contribute to directional strategy. Responsibilities: Support the analysis and submission of reports to various constituencies (local regulators) based on local statutory reporting. Manage Local Regulatory Reporting requirements for Central Bank of UAE, Dubai Financial Service Authority, Financial Service Regulatory Authority and Qatar Financial Centre Regulatory Authority. Submission of Local Financial Statements in liaison with external auditors Responsible for Capital and Liquidity management reporting including but not limited to: - Internal Capital Adequacy Assessment Process (ICAAP), - Stress Testing - Basel, Pillar III requirements - Recovery Plan. Assist with closing the books and sub-ledgers, including passing appropriate required adjustment entries and consolidating financials at the end of each accounting period Instill operational discipline into existing team processes Work with senior leadership on strategic planning, establishing guidelines, implementing controls, and executing strategic plans Assist with the implementation of policies and procedures Serve as a technical mentor for an area within the business Build and maintain a thorough understanding of related product reporting and analytics and utilize knowledge of systems and process flows to provide insight and recommendations on identified issues Has the ability to operate with a limited level of direct supervision. Can exercise independence of judgement and autonomy. Acts as SME to senior stakeholders and /or other team members. Appropriately assess risk when business decisions are made, demonstrating particular consideration for the firm's reputation and safeguarding Citigroup, its clients and assets, by driving compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, adhering to Policy, applying sound ethical judgment regarding personal behavior, conduct and business practices, and escalating, managing and reporting control issues with transparency. Qualifications: 6-10 years of experience in Financial Services preferred Global Project Management experience preferred Ability to work under pressure and manage deadlines or unexpected changes in expectations or requirements Proven organizational and time management skills Proven problem-solving and decision-making skills Consistently demonstrates clear and concise written and verbal communication skills Education: Bachelor’s Degree/University degree or equivalent experience Master’s degree preferred This job description provides a high-level review of the types of work performed. Other job-related duties may be assigned as required. #LI-ZA1 ------------------------------------------------------ Job Family Group: Finance ------------------------------------------------------ Job Family: Finance Management ------------------------------------------------------ Time Type: Full time ------------------------------------------------------ Most Relevant Skills Assessment, Budgeting, Business Acumen, Credible Challenge, Financial Acumen, Management Reporting, Planning and Organizing, Problem Solving, Risk Management, Stakeholder Management. ------------------------------------------------------ Other Relevant Skills For complementary skills, please see above and/or contact the recruiter. ------------------------------------------------------ Citi is an equal opportunity employer, and qualified candidates will receive consideration without regard to their race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any other characteristic pro …

jobs.title: 2025 Wealth Management Summer Internship, Dubai, UAE (UAE Nationals preferred)
jobs.description: The ten week summer analyst program starts in June and begins with an introduction to Citi and Corporate Banking. You will then join a team where you will gain a real-hands on experience by working on a number of live projects as well as attending speaker presentations. High performing summer analysts will be considered for a full time position in 2026 after graduation. Your time here will look something like this... Supporting the team in identifying and executing complex complex problems Analyzing transaction flows of the world's largest financial institutions Be a part of the front end of technological development for the financial transaction space. Work and support more than 50 different geographies across the region to deliver the best solutions to our clients. Work with Private Bankers to identify prospects and present value propositions offered by fiduciary offshore structures (trusts, investment holding vehicles and insurance solutions) to high-net-worth clients of Citi Private Bank (CPB). Attend client meetings with Bankers and participate in presentations and sales pitches We want to hear from you if... You are due to graduate in 2026 On course to achieve a 3.0 GPA out of 4.0 (or equivalent) at undergraduate level in any degree Keen interest in a career in financial services Who we think will be a great fit... We’re looking for motivated individuals, who are eager to start their careers at a global organization, tend to think globally, and have an innovative outlook on business. We know that anyone, not just those with business degrees can have a knack for business, so we’re open to all disciplines and candidates with diverse work experience. We value diversity and so do you. We’ll also be looking for the following: Excellent interpersonal skills to deal internally and externally with clients. Strong analytical and Presentation skills Desire to develop a deep understanding of the financial industry Intellectual curiosity and proactive approach to searching for new and creative ideas Articulate, strong communication, planning, and organisational skills You also have a strong desire to learn, are proactive and good at building rapport and relationships in team environments. Unquestioned commitment to integrity ethical decision-making We review applications on a rolling basis and therefore encourage you to apply as soon as possible. ------------------------------------------------------ Job Family Group: Private Client Coverage ------------------------------------------------------ Job Family: Development Program ------------------------------------------------------ Time Type: Full time ------------------------------------------------------ Citi is an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to their race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran. Citigroup Inc. and its subsidiaries ("Citi”) invite all qualified interested applicants to apply for career opportunities. If you are a person with a disability and need a reasonable accommodation to use our search tools and/or apply for a career opportunity review Accessibility at Citi . jobs.title: Senior Managing Consultant, Services Business Development
jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Senior Managing Consultant, Services Business Development Job Description Summary Director / Senior Managing Consultant, Services Business Development Overview The Services Business Development team is a key differentiator for Mastercard, providing the cutting-edge services that help our customers grow. Focused on thinking big and scaling fast around the globe, this agile team is responsible for end-to-end solutions for a diverse global customer base. Centered on data-driven technologies and innovation, these services include payments-focused consulting, loyalty and marketing programs, Test & Learn experimentation, and data-driven information and risk management services. Client verticals include Financial Institutions, Retail & Commerce and Digital Partners, Governments, and many others. Role Senior Managing Consultant role responsible for driving revenue for UAE business. Partner with large team who works with Mastercard account teams and clients, and who helps ensure effective delivery of all engagements across the markets. • Responsible for developing and delivering our in-region sales strategy and directing all business development for with direct revenue responsibility • Deliver against aggressive sales goals • Act as a Player-Coach with expectation to travel extensively to help selling process and coach team • Be capable of developing and maintaining relationships with customers at the most senior levels • Strong communication and influencing skills, should be a compelling presenter to senior client audiences and able to influence large deals • Excellent problem solving and analytics skills and should be able to go head-to-head and influence internal stakeholders and customers alike with fact-based thinking • Will serve as the face of Mastercard Services to senior level client stakeholders, including c-suite • Will partner with leadership to develop a plan for Services to support their strategic imperatives • Facilitate the implementation of strategies and action plans which, in turn, will act as a link between customer needs and Mastercard resources • Build, recruit, manage, develop, and coach a team responsible for each different customer’s business opportunities to increase revenue, share and volume • Partner closely with all product teams to ensure customer needs and product innovations align with development road maps • Partner closely with Advisors & Consulting Services and Finance teams to drive and actively track delivery and revenue • Translate objectives into key hypotheses and structure work for large/complex projects or ill-defined problems All About You • Deep understanding of the suite of Mastercard Advisors value propositions with a thoughtful approach to executing against a defined territory • Strong commercial drive with ability to build and monetize senior client relationships based on empathy, thought leadership and subject matter expertise • Preferably strong consulting and / or data driven solution selling background • Strong networker across relevant stakeholder base with the ability to listen, build common ground and influence to effectively advance business opportunities and generate revenues • Proven track record of progressively complex sales and strategic account management experience within the financial services sector; payments industry experience is preferred • Demonstrated successful account management experience • Revenue management and P&L experience • Successful con …

jobs.title: Director, Business Development, Corporate Solutions - B2B Acceptance
jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Director, Business Development, Corporate Solutions - B2B Acceptance Overview This role sits within Corporate Solutions and is responsible for developing and scaling Mastercard’s B2B Acceptance strategy across East + West Arabia and Eastern Europe. You will help acquirers, PSPs, fintechs and merchants unlock new commercial payment flows. B2B Acceptance is a significant growth opportunity. Corporate buyers increasingly want to pay suppliers using commercial and virtual cards, particularly for invoice-based payments. For acquirers and merchants, this creates an opportunity to capture new high-value flows, improve cash flow, reduce manual processes and enable better reconciliation through data and straight-through processing. Key questions • Do you have a track record of building and scaling commercial payment products or acceptance-led growth strategies? • Can you influence senior executives across banks, acquirers, PSPs, fintechs and large merchants? • Are you motivated by solving commercial and operational problems through technology, data and partnerships? • Can you turn market trends into execution plans that drive GDV, revenue and merchant activation? Role • Develop and execute the EEMEA B2B Acceptance strategy to retain, grow and expand Mastercard’s commercial acceptance footprint. • Drive incremental commercial volume and revenue by enabling acquirers, PSPs, fintechs and merchants to capture high-value B2B and invoice-based payment flows. • Remove operational barriers to B2B acceptance, including onboarding friction, manual processing, reconciliation challenges, supplier education and acceptance visibility. • Build and scale market strategies for virtual card acceptance, supplier enablement, straight-through processing and automated reconciliation. • Provide thought leadership to global product teams on B2B merchant, acquiring, fintech, PSP, AP/AR, ERP and embedded finance opportunities. • Lead digital presence, market insights, research and sales enablement for Commercial Acceptance across Eastern Europe and the Middle East. • Build educational collateral and go-to-market playbooks to support banks, acquirers, fintechs, merchants and internal teams. • Partner with Commercial Solutions, issuers, acquirers, PSPs, fintechs and account teams to activate commercial acceptance assets and partnerships. • Manage dashboards and KPIs covering merchant activation, supplier enablement, GDV, net revenue, pipeline conversion and partner performance. • Ensure Mastercard resources are deployed effectively to support commercial outcomes, market priorities and scalable regional growth. All About You • Strong understanding of acquiring, merchant economics, commercial card acceptance, virtual cards and B2B payment flows. • Proven ability to build and scale product value propositions, particularly across acquiring, merchant or supplier enablement. • Experience in Accounts Receivable, Accounts Payable, ERP, procurement, embedded finance or invoice-based payments is a strong advantage. • Strong commercial mindset, with experience managing revenue growth, pipeline execution, business performance or P&L-related outcomes. • Executive presence and consultative sales experience, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders internally and externally. • Strong analytical capability, including the ability to use data to build business cases, track performance and shape strategy. • Collaborative, entrepreneurial and execution-f …

jobs.title: Splunk Solutions Engineer (Observability) - Dubai
jobs.description: The application window is expected to close on: June 30th, 2026 Note: Job posting may be removed earlier if the position is filled or if a sufficient number of applications are received. Note: Candidates must be based in Dubai, UAE. Meet the Team Splunk, a Cisco company, is building a resilient digital world with an end-to-end platform designed for hybrid, multi-cloud environments. We approach our work with kindness, valuing the unique talents, passion, and joy that each team member brings to our collaborative culture. Our team is dedicated to helping organizations reach their best by solving complex business challenges at scale. We foster an environment of inclusiveness, disruption, and fun, ensuring that every member feels supported as they reach new heights. Your Impact As an Observability Solutions Engineer, you play a crucial role in the pre-sales process, partnering with Sales to deliver technical expertise on Splunk’s observability solutions. Establish and proactively maintain positive relationships with clients and partners. Collaborate closely with account teams to align with sales strategies, providing support through briefings, demos, proposals, Proof of Values (POVs), integrations, and issue resolution for Splunk’s observability portfolio. Engage directly with customers to understand their IT challenges and business objectives, mapping these to Splunk product capabilities. Demonstrate Splunk’s observability products and services onsite, remotely via webinars, and at field events such as conferences and trade shows. Apply organized and analytical approaches to overcome sales obstacles creatively and adaptively. Contribute to a dynamic technical organization with a rapidly growing customer base. Minimum Qualifications Experience in technical sales, pre-sales, or solution engineering within the Enterprise Software, ITOps or Cybersecurity sector. Experience delivering technical product demonstrations to customers in person, via webinars, or at industry events. Prior experience with Splunk, AppDynamics, or equivalent APM (Application Performance Monitoring) technology stack, alongside a data platform such as Splunk Cloud/Enterprise. Experience conducting proof of value engagements leveraging observability tools, including Splunk AppDynamics, for application performance and hybrid monitoring. Experience with modern application architecture, including Kubernetes, Docker, or serverless technologies. Knowledge of digital experience monitoring, including synthetics and real-user monitoring tools. Preferred Qualifications Strong business acumen with the ability to understand organizational IT, cybersecurity and compliance structures and digital transformation drivers. Familiarity with Splunk Observability Cloud, AppDynamics or equivalent platform architecture, deployment, configuration, and troubleshooting, including setting up baselines, health rules, and proactive monitoring strategies. Proficiency in modern programming languages such as Python, Java, or Go. Knowledge of AI tools and concepts, including LLMs, GenAI frameworks, or Time Series models. Knowledge of security operation standards. Attainment of Splunk Enterprise Security Admin certification or other cybersecurity industry certifications (i.e., CISSP, SANS Cybersecurity, etc.) Splunk AppDynamics/Observability Cloud certifications are a plus. Willingness to travel to visit prospects and engage in onsite customer work as required. Why Cisco? At Cisco, we’re revolutionizing how data and infrastructure connect and protect organizations in the AI era – and beyond. We’ve been innovating fearlessly for 40 years to create solutions that power how humans and technology work together across the physical and digital worlds. These solutions provide customers with unparalleled security, visibility, and insights across the entire digital footprint. Fueled by the depth and breadth of our technology, we experiment and create meaningful solutions. Add to that our worldwide network …

jobs.title: Splunk Solutions Engineer - Dubai

Note: Candidates must be based in Dubai, UAE. Meet the Team Splunk, a Cisco company, is building a resilient digital world with an end-to-end platform designed for hybrid, multi-cloud environments. We approach our work with kindness, valuing the unique talents, passion, and joy that each team member brings to our collaborative culture. Willingness to travel to visit prospects and engage in onsite customer work as required. Prior experience with Splunk data platform, SecOps/ITOps/APM or requivalent technology stack Hands-on experience with conducting proof of value engagements leveraging data platforms, security operations, or observability tools. Preferred Qualifications Strong business acumen with the ability to understand organizational IT, cybsecurity and compliance structures and digital transformation drivers. Curiosity-driven mindset with a focus on uncovering the "why" behind technical scenarios and customer pain points. Knowledge of security operation standards. Attainment of Splunk Enterprise Security Admin certification or other cybersecurity industry certifications (i.e., CISSP, SANS Cybsecurity, etc.) Experience with modern application architecture, including Kubernetes, Docker, or serverless technologies. Knowledge of digital experience monitoring, including synthetics and real-user monitoring tools. AppDynamics/Observability Cloud certifications are a plus. Proficiency in modern programming languages such as Python, Java, or Go. Knowledge of AI tools and concepts, including LLMs, GenAI frameworks, or Time Series models. Why Cisco? At Cisco, we’re revolutionizing how data and infrastructure connect and protect organizations in the AI era – and beyond. We work as a team, collaborating with empathy to make really big things happen on a global scale. Because our solutions are everywhere, our impact is everywhere. We are Cisco, and our power starts with you.

jobs.title: Executive Clinical Director, Pharmacy - Cleveland Clinic Abu Dhabi
jobs.description: At Cleveland Clinic Health System, we believe in a better future for healthcare. And each of us is responsible for honoring our commitment to excellence, pushing the boundaries and transforming the patient experience, every day. We all have the power to help, heal and change lives — beginning with our own. That’s the power of the Cleveland Clinic Health System team, and The Power of Every One. Job Title Executive Clinical Director, Pharmacy - Cleveland Clinic Abu Dhabi Location Facility Cleveland Clinic Abu Dhabi Department Job Code T97812 Shift Days Schedule 8:00am-5:00pm Job Summary This position requires relocation to Abu Dhabi. The Executive Clinical Director, Pharmacy provides high level leadership, strategic direction and advocacy in the management of Pharmacy services at CCAD. Reporting to the CMO, the Executive Clinical Director, Pharmacy develops the overall strategy and plan for the support and delivery of operations of pharmacy programs and services to achieve the vision for CCAD, including outcomes and workforce issues at a strategic and tactical level. This position is also responsible for planning, directing and coordinating the activities of the pharmacy to ensure efficient, innovative and cost-effective pharmacy service. Job Details Primary Job Responsibilities: Leads the development, implementation and evaluation of the annual strategic plan and ensures effective mechanisms for the involvement of the Division’s leadership team in decision making Provides strategic leadership in facilitating compliance with operational standards and regulations, through assessment and monitoring of Pharmacy practices, processes and systems Leads the application of digital and artificial intelligence to support innovative pharmacy services Responsible for all operational aspects of the Inpatient and Outpatient pharmacy, including leading and continuously enhancing the effectiveness of Patient and Provider Access functions for the Cleveland Clinic. Plans, implements and manages the provision of pharmaceutical care in all pharmacies and patient care areas Coordinates operations among pharmacies and patient care areas to ensure appropriate levels of program offerings, access and consistent levels of service quality, regulatory compliance and financial performance. Ensures the cost-effectiveness of pharmacy service provisions and operations throughout CCAD. Ensures consistent compliance with applicable legislative, regulatory and/or accreditation standards relating to pharmaceuticals within pharmacies and other patient care areas. Enables the appropriate placement, facilitation and expedition of all pharmacy flow transactions, including direct and emergency patient admissions and transfers, in accordance with established clinical standards. Oversees the preparation and dispensing of medications. Develops standards and takes necessary actions to ensure the consistency and quality of pharmaceutical care and customer service. Works with patients and professional staff to design and implement therapy plans to produce specific therapeutic outcomes for patients. Manages purchasing activities to ensure adequate supplies and drug inventory, adhering to regulatory and financial compliance. Ensures adequate staffing levels to provide safe and effective pharmacy services. Reviews and advocate changes jointly with Quality Management team on how to implement them and provide training, tools and techniques to enable caregivers to achieve quality standards and practice in a safe manner. Contributes to the development of the strategic and operational planning across CCAD in line with regional Healthcare strategy and Health Authority Abu Dhabi. Work closely with Hospital, Administration, Physicians and other key stakeholders, to identify new and emerging approaches to enhancing pharmacy access for patients. Responsible for executing the overall direction and strategy to build strong centralized support teams. Organize functions of department through …

jobs.title: Field Service Engineer - Centrifugal Compressor
jobs.location: AE-ABU DHABI-AL GHAITH YARD, MUSSAFAH INDUSTRIAL 16th STREET
jobs.description: Field Service Engineer - Centrifugal Compressor Are you a Mechanical Field Service Engineer looking for an interesting and inspiring opportunity? Would you like to be part of our Turbomachinery Process Solutions Product Line? Join our Industrial and Energy Technology Team! Our Industrial and Energy Technology (IET) operates across 120 countries and serves the entire energy value chain and industrial sector. We hold an extensive portfolio of rotating equipment, process flow, transmission technology and lifecycle services. Our Engineering Transformation teamwork to improve the quality of our Engineering processes. Partner with the best As a Field Service Engineer - Centrifugal Compressors, you will be coordinating and overseeing work at the field site, providing quality service to the customer. In addition, your Job role may include providing the planning necessary for the job including instructions to the crew and equipment. As a Field Service Engineer - Centrifugal Compressor, you will be responsible for Providing technical advice and support to clients and craft-labour in the installation, commissioning and maintenance of Baker Hughes-supplied Centrifugal Compressors. Unloading and Inspection of the Centrifugal Compressor & its major auxiliaries at the installation site and their placement on the foundation. Improving Geometric dimensional check of foundation, equipment and auxiliary skids positioning, levelling, and alignment Performing Installation, commissioning & Startup of various types of Centrifugal Compressors MCL, BCL, PCL, and SRL supplied by Baker Hughes at customer sites. Performing minor & major inspections of Centrifugal Compressors. Involving assembly, disassembly, checking and adjusting clearances evaluating wear and damage state of Couplings, lubricated-tooth, and dry-elastic types, thrust bearings and journal bearings of various kinds, gas seals, labyrinth and honeycomb types, Oil seals, gauged floating rings types, Dry gas seals or oil mechanical seals, Labyrinth or honeycomb inner seals, Diaphragms or diaphragms bundle and volutes. Fuel your passion To be successful in this role you will: Have a bachelor’s in engineering with 5+ years of relevant industry experience. Have working experience in Rotating equipment in the Field at Customer Sites. Have excellent Functional/Technical, Customer Focus, Communication, Interpersonal, and Team working skills. Have prior experience in Installation and/or Maintenance activities of Centrifugal Compressors in the Field at Customer Sites Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. In this role, we can offer the following flexible working patterns: When assignments are complete, in general, you won't be required to visit the office, however, you can operate from Home and perform basic tasks like completing previous assignment reports, mobilization-related aspects, etc before you take on your next projects. The company shall ensure a work-life balance for the individual. Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. Join us, and you can expect: Contemporary work-life balance policies and well-being activities Comprehensive private medical care options The safety net of life insurance and disability programs Tailored financial programs Additional elected or voluntary benefits T …

jobs.title: Field Engineer Supervisor - Wireline
jobs.description: Field Supervisor – Wireline Would you like the opportunity to learn cutting-edge technology? Do you enjoy being part of a successful team? Join our Wireline Team! We are the leader in well construction and production. Our innovative physical and digital solutions improve efficiency, production and maximize reservoir value. Partner with the best Baker Hughes is seeking an experienced and dynamic Field Supervisor for our Wireline services, specifically focusing on open hole logging operations. The ideal candidate will have significant hands-on experience in wireline operations, with a preference for those who have worked with Baker Hughes products. In this role, you will lead a team of field operators, ensuring the safe and efficient execution of wireline services while maintaining the highest standards of quality and customer satisfaction. As a Field Supervisor - Wireline , you will be responsible for: Under broad direction leads crews and equipment at the workshop and wellsite in the execution of wireline logging and completions services Minor maintenance of equipment and assisting in actuating the completions equipment. Working with the Customer Representative and drill crew on location to coordinate operations at the rig. Providing a record of all assemblies as run at the well site to the Customer Representative and promotes and maintains good customer relations. Being primary contact to work with the Customer Representative advising them on the current challenges at the wellsite exerting significant latitude in determining how to best troubleshoot the well. Applying Health, Safety and Environment (HS&E) standards and procedures in all situations to ensure compliance is maintained. Leading and developing a project resources team to successfully execute strategies for the management of customer projects. Showing flexibility and be adaptable to changes to accommodate operations timing, travel requirements and hitch duration. ​ Fuel your passion To be successful in this role you will: Have a minimum of 7 years of experience in wireline operations, with a strong focus on open hole logging. Proven experience with Baker Hughes wireline products and technologies is a must. Be happy to travel to international locations to work with customers on site. Have experience in prior field-based roles. Show the ability to adapt and change in a fast-moving, dynamic environment. Have an excellent awareness of Health, Safety and Environmental compliance and potential risks. Have excellent leadership skills and the ability to manage and motivate a diverse team in a dynamic work environment. Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone. Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. Join us, and you can expect: · Contemporary work-life balance policies and wellbeing activities Comprehensive private medical care options Safety net of life insurance and disability programs Tailored financial programs Additional elected or voluntary benefits The Baker Hughes internal title for this role is: FIELD SUPV I - WLE

jobs.title: Oil & Gas Sales Specialist - UAE
jobs.description: Bring your energy to Aggreko as Oil & Gas Sales Specialist based in Abu Dhabi. You will have a sales plan (account portfolio) where you will manage and deliver growth within your current customers , target and win back your lapsed customers , and prospect target customers (new clients) with a focus on growth coupled with profitability. First class people skills are essential as well as a tenacity to succeed and be professional in all that you do. The role is 40%/60% split between new business prospecting and existing account growth. What you’ll do as Oil & Gas Sales Specialist - Abu Dhabi: Responsible for targeting, developing & acquiring customers within your sales plan. Motivated to meet and exceed targets. Revenue targets and sales KPI driven. Ensure 3rd party costs are appropriately priced. (Fuel, Freight & Rehire) Establishes and maintain effective working relationships with customers and colleagues. Process driven, following Aggreko’s sales process (sector sales excellence), as well as all CRM and CPQ processes. (salesforce) Minimum sales activity requirements and CRM & CPQ compliance requirements. Develop and execute a sales and marketing plan for developing business, including product / sector revenue targets. Identify new customers / business with a view to growing key account portfolio. To ensure that the network of current and potential customers is expanded and maintained through weekly external face to face and telephone sales calls. Prepare and submit proposals that include technical and commercial details of the client’s needs in accordance with Aggreko’s terms and conditions. To acquire a sound knowledge of internal administrative practice, including all hire procedures and the quick track system, product knowledge and the operation of the depot overall. You are: Experience within the temporary power & HVAC / Oil & Gas industry Degree qualification in sales or in a technical discipline OR Minimum 5 years of successful high-level sales experience in the industrial services environment. Dedication and commitment to achieving objectives. Experience of selling (or renting) capital equipment or selling a technical / engineering solutions or services. ( Solution Selling ) Proven track record in delivering results in line with targets set. Oil & Gas experience in the UAE is a plus. Here’s what you’ll get: Competitive compensation package Industry-leading benefit plans including medical insurance Continuous training and development with career growth opportunities Safety-focused culture Our people are can-do, positive, resilient and persistent. If that feels like you, apply now and build your career with the people bringing energy to the world. #LI-MK1 Equal employment opportunity We welcome people from different backgrounds and cultures, and respect people’s unique skills, attitudes and experiences. We encourage everyone to be themselves at work because we know that’s how we do our best, for each other, for our customers, for the communities where we work, and for our careers. We are an equal opportunity employer. If you apply for a role at Aggreko, we will consider your application based on your qualifications and experience, and not on your race, colour, ethnicity, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or veteran status.

jobs.title: Construction Manager - Rail
jobs.description: Job Responsibilities Manage and oversee all construction activities related to the programme, ensuring compliance with approved designs, specifications, quality standards, and contractual requirements. Lead and supervise the site supervision team and monitor contractor performance, progress, and resource deployment. Coordinate with contractors, stakeholders, utility providers, and authorities to ensure efficient project delivery. Identify and mitigate construction risks, resolve site issues, and support timely decision making. Ensure compliance with health, safety, environmental, and quality requirements. Monitor project progress and support the successful testing, commissioning, and handover of works. Minimum Requirements Minimum 15 years of experience in the management of design and/or construction of major infrastructure projects. Proven experience as a Construction Manager on at least one project of similar size and complexity. Experience managing multidisciplinary construction and site supervision teams. Strong knowledge of construction management, contract administration, quality assurance, and health and safety requirements. Middle East experience is highly advantageous.   Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering or a related discipline from an accredited university. Master's degree is preferred. Chartered Engineer status or equivalent professional accreditation is highly desirable.

jobs.title: Inspector - Material
jobs.description: Inspect site conditions prior to start of construction activities. Verify the compatibility of the site with the designed permanent works. Check for obstructions. Photograph and document the condition of existing property that might be damaged by the work. Make sure all existing structures, property, utilities and landscaping are properly protected. Review materials delivered to site for conformance with approved samples, materials and equipment and report findings to RE. Check that materials delivered to site are properly stored and report discrepancies to the RE. Inspect the construction of temporary and permanent works to ensure compliance with the Contract Documents and approved shop drawings and submittals Prepare daily logs and reports. Maintain records of inspections made, tests performed, work executed, etc perform all work in accordance with the Contract Administration Procedures Manual. Observe site safety practices and the progress of the work. Report any concerns to the RE. Assists with the inspection of completed works. Help prepare punch lists. Monitor the start-up, testing, commissioning and handover of the works. Review RFIs received from Contractor (s) and make recommendations on resolution. Review any requests for variance received from Contractors and provide comments as to entitlement and recommendations on resolution. Able to read working drawings and related specifications; be able to enforce these specifications and produce relevant reports. Bachelor’s Degree or Diploma in Relevant Technical Field. RTA or DM approval is must

jobs.title: Engineer - Planning
jobs.description: Develop, review, and maintain detailed project schedules (baseline, recovery, and revised programs) using Primavera P6 or equivalent planning software. Prepare master schedules, look-ahead programs, and weekly/monthly progress reports in coordination with project teams. Monitor project progress against the approved baseline and identify delays, risks, and critical path impacts. Perform schedule risk analysis, delay analysis, and time impact analysis (TIA) in accordance with contract requirements. Coordinate with construction, procurement, design, and commercial teams to ensure alignment between schedule, resources, and deliverables. Review contractors’ planning submissions, method statements, and manpower histograms for compliance with contract specifications. Support the Project Manager/Resident Engineer by providing planning inputs for decision-making and corrective actions. Prepare and evaluate EOT (Extension of Time) claims, substantiating delays with proper planning narratives and evidence. Track procurement, long-lead items, approvals, and permits to ensure schedule compliance. Develop recovery and mitigation plans to bring delayed activities back on track. Conduct weekly and monthly planning meetings with stakeholders and issue meeting minutes related to schedule actions. Ensure integration of cost, cash flow, and resource-loaded schedules where required. Maintain proper documentation and ensure planning records comply with project controls and QA/QC procedures. Provide mentoring and guidance to junior planning engineers and site planners. Liaise with clients, consultants, and authorities on all schedule-related matters. Support project close-out by preparing as-built programs and final planning reports.

jobs.title: Material Inspector (Roads and Infrastructure)
jobs.description: • Inspect and verify all construction materials delivered to site to ensure compliance with RTA specifications, approved material submittals, and contract requirements . • Check material source approvals, delivery notes, batch certificates, and test reports prior to acceptance and use at site. • Carry out sampling and witnessing of laboratory and field tests for soil, aggregates, concrete, asphalt, steel, and other civil materials as per approved ITPs. • Ensure materials used for roadworks, bridges, culverts, drainage, and utility works conform to approved specifications and drawings. • Monitor storage, handling, and protection of materials to prevent contamination, segregation, or damage. • Verify compliance with RTA-approved suppliers, manufacturers, and testing laboratories . • Coordinate with the Material Engineer and Site Engineer regarding testing schedules, approvals, and material-related issues. • Inspect asphalt paving, concrete works, earthworks, and backfilling to ensure correct materials and workmanship are used. • Review and submit Work Inspection Requests (WIRs) related to materials and civil works. • Maintain accurate inspection records, test results, and material logs for QA/QC and as-built documentation. • Identify non-conforming materials, raise NCRs and site observations , and follow up on corrective actions. • Ensure compliance with HSE requirements , especially during material handling, sampling, and testing activities. • Monitor material usage and quantities to avoid wastage, misuse, or deviation from approved specifications . • Assist in RTA inspections and audits , providing required documentation and site support. • Report daily site activities, material inspections, and testing status to the Material Engineer / Resident Engineer . Bachelor’s Degree or Diploma in Relevant Technical Field RTA/ DCL  approval is preferred 7+ years of relevant experience in laboratory materials testing including field inspection of samples and materials. Middle East experience preferred Relevant experience in comparable roads and bridges projects
